**Look around, Peter. Right now, in America...**
_Children are being taught in many schools to be ashamed of their country and themselves._

_The pledge is being taken out of school because it mentions God._

_Free speech is being censored and silenced right before our eyes._

_Democrats are fighting for the right to elective abortion right up to the moment of birth._

_Unelected, unaccountable bureaucrats are seeking control of almost every aspect of our lives._

**As Speaker of the House, my mission is clear: we must elect more leaders who make decisions based on American values with an emphasis on God, family, and country. **

As we aim to make history in the 2024 Election, I need to have my hand on the pulse of the most important thing: _**American voters.**_

[**Which is why I am conducting a _critical_ American Voter Survey today.**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=d3631cc1fd024b3cb148a070329a9d4c&_e=c49Yyjv1VjJGK6xARKgr7zHOqBY6-TYUBAss99xt0vsVfc8eS9gHpoABTq2WLouH1Ebt_XiV1QMbr06507QR-eZdBJ3FyK2MOBblB9ZF-JezE7pjBbzXf-Exix-o768Ub5ak-ogn0Y16GUc_F4NcaUC6Z4pNwGKMCDEUL0z4tiB57gU82mzbCGd_-bBq5H1cmj7V394-v6a63GFZNR2zfDJEkMReY06pMUQICHCj4_t7BX4yAWGNIlYq_KidCzuEcFw0vS08Q_VYl_BKFEpQzfv1wo-OTGwSeqdI5jt9g_-p2_mzFNZ4w1FBNgDSHWRH-5FOQZ1vI-9lV_UElOAe1CKoO2UIIYSoIcuNdwK9k1YMYgYA5YuLv7LTq9ruWNP9YEOfovSdie8wV4DRuneoBAj7FOn3b6ocHDBwcvpNyv0%3D)

She was an enthusiastic delegate for his campaign, lending her voice and influence to his re-election. = In 2022, thanks to Supreme Court nominees appointed by Donald Trump during his presidency, Roe v. body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?refcode=120823 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?refcode=120823]
Friend,
Thank you. Because of DFLers like you, we were able to win in the House District 52B special election, and Representative-Elect Bianca Virnig will be serving in the Minnesota House.
Grassroots support fuels our campaigns. From donations to door-knocking, you make a DFL House majority possible. This House DFL majority and DFL trifecta have shown how much good we can accomplish for Minnesotans: protecting abortion access, historic investments in infrastructure, strengthening public education, creating a paid family and medical leave program, and so much more. We’re working hard to grow the economy by growing Minnesota’s middle class, lowering costs, and reducing stress in people’s lives.
We still have work to do, friend. Can I count on you to support the House DFL by making a donation today?If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Donate $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=120823]
Donate $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=120823]
Donate $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=120823]
Donate $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=120823]
Or, another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-email2023?express_lane_other=true&refcode=120823]
Thank you for your support, friend. 2024 is right around the corner, and we need to be prepared for extreme MAGA Republican efforts to flip the Minnesota House. Protecting our House DFL Majority is key to building a Minnesota that works better for everyone and reflects the values that we all share. I look forward to working towards that goal with you.
Melissa Hortman
Speaker of the House
Minnesota House of Representatives

body:
My fellow Americans - this is Mike Johnson.

| |

---
| | | My fellow Americans - this is Mike Johnson.

_**[This is a longer email but I'm humbly asking you to read it start to finish.](http://links.fighttheswamp.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=cfd5c8d996754cd1af97417bb36b9e1a&_e=-A5AmICtoS3zIHpQvkRHqEfbEtig-SRb9g4bjquP_bLgxAeHG0jdg0XwthSRJyi9gGmbWYVuvdPsU1s_Gi1VNsWFsqtiRN4LwxYzwJglcsabk-lVRaM-7RWt5VESnhkr_FXfBXiy8KLS-HN4QD2cuZzO37a4XXu18sHd9Q0AXnNHDRFzquE5Lv89PBMorSp_go_2FHoetdjuSe-iXEcEqwY-1PlPRLsXvKVxKNAjxCyRGHojW7MKrv-00abjusl5lQ1kNrqxIYXthpmDv2orcUMUTN5Fax5cGLkFa-XJNcHW7jfswisRVpjp1emuPFpF0tyS_2fGaCUsO89YovKKpDSHjlFX5OPMMmEwJkaei_sbEUJRaQs3YrE01TfNasd3lVeUWa8_5eyJi0DJCbQxkzprt5c5MI01FE-VzAdZd8I%3D "This is a longer email but I’m humbly asking you to read it start to finish.")**_

Why? Because the moment I was elected Speaker by my Republican colleagues, the Radical Left IMMEDIATELY unleashed a coordinated, preplanned political hit job against me.
* The Liberal Media spun up hit piece after hit piece to smear me in the press - Biden's former Press Secretary even attacked me for being a Christian on MSNBC.
* Politico fired off this headline: _" Democrats rev up the opposition machine against Mike Johnson."_
* And I can't even begin to tell you how many Democrat fundraising emails and texts I saw in the minutes following the final vote - I'm bracing myself for the next FEC reports.

Look:

These vicious attacks are a dime a dozen in Washington. They don't intimidate me. _But I 'll let you in on a little secret: that's not _really_ the goal..._

The _real_ goal of this political ambush is two-fold: **1)** smear the good names of every House Republican to hurt them electorally, and **2)** scare Liberals enough to whip out their credit cards, fill in their info, and donate to Democrats.

**It 's about power and money. Nothing else.**

That's why it's mission-critical that we stand up for ourselves in critical moments like this. _**[So as one of my first moves as Speaker, I'm teaming up with my fellow Louisianian - Majority Leader Steve Scalise - to set a goal of raising one million dollars between now and the end of the month.](http://links.fighttheswamp.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=cfd5c8d996754cd1af97417bb36b9e1a&_e=-A5AmICtoS3zIHpQvkRHqEfbEtig-SRb9g4bjquP_bLgxAeHG0jdg0XwthSRJyi9gGmbWYVuvdPsU1s_Gi1VNsWFsqtiRN4LwxYzwJglcsabk-lVRaM-7RWt5VESnhkr_FXfBXiy8KLS-HN4QD2cuZzO37a4XXu18sHd9Q0AXnNHDRFzquE5Lv89PBMorSp_go_2FHoetdjuSe-iXEcEqwY-1PlPRLsXvKVxKNAjxCyRGHojW7MKrv-00abjusl5lQ1kNrqxIYXthpmDv2orcTjWMGRtPGCCuJY5sN06HiXFzrhFBUgnb6GCaIbwodKNqCSQZQylyoHHAh443lL2QF8QBgOkcsqHjNomxXavNTEiOz2lbYvPuH-TDOR5wbrSmEBNFXj0Y9iwxDBwu1AVfn75XZVK5janXKouOJa2Bks%3D "So as one of my first moves as Speaker, I’m teaming up with my fellow Louisianian – Majority Leader Steve Scalise – to set a goal of raising one million dollars between now and the end of the month.")**_

It's ambitious. I get it. But that's the bare minimum we need to fully fund our campaign operations in battleground races across the country.

**If we fall short of that, we 'll be forced to SLASH our voter contact budgets, GUT our digital outreach efforts, and pull our ads OFF THE AIRWAVES. Nothing could be worse for our plans to defend and expand our slim House Majority.**

So here's my final pitch to you:

We urgently need your help. Not next week, not in the new year - right here and right now.

The difference between a strong Conservative Majority and one controlled by the Liberal Machine will come down to can't-miss fundraising goals just like this.

Let me make myself perfectly clear:

_I 'm not asking you to pitch in more than you can afford. If you can spare $50 or $100, I would be tremendously grateful. But if you can only spare $5 or $10, I would be equally appreciative._

_**[The ONLY thing I ask is for every Conservative in the country to chip in SOMETHING.](http://links.fighttheswamp.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=cfd5c8d996754cd1af97417bb36b9e1a&_e=-A5AmICtoS3zIHpQvkRHqEfbEtig-SRb9g4bjquP_bLgxAeHG0jdg0XwthSRJyi9gGmbWYVuvdPsU1s_Gi1VNsWFsqtiRN4LwxYzwJglcsabk-lVRaM-7RWt5VESnhkr_FXfBXiy8KLS-HN4QD2cuZzO37a4XXu18sHd9Q0AXnNHDRFzquE5Lv89PBMorSp_go_2FHoetdjuSe-iXEcEqwY-1PlPRLsXvKVxKNAjxCyRGHojW7MKrv-00abjusl5lQ1kNrqxIYXthpmDv2orcaHcTq5RWJH9Fzz696Y-P1NvV1kkFwuD_W-si0ydxjQApf9eqTbgIWNAuYdlylpC1TCXVEiJVCTvody_QKuuoghXoVcQ06WBkSvVufKGMA8UtjV3Ve75fOQK3z4WIatjfOFuihZaAS8OP6jEFd8Pqbk%3D "The ONLY thing I ask is for every Conservative in the country to chip in SOMETHING.")**_

That will be the difference between victory and defeat. So what do you say? Can I count you in?

---
Thanks for all you do.

In liberty,
Speaker Mike Johnson

clean_body: My fellow Americans - this is Mike Johnson. | | | | | My fellow Americans - this is Mike Johnson. "This is a longer email but I’m humbly asking you to read it start to finish." Why? Because the moment I was elected Speaker by my Republican colleagues, the Radical Left IMMEDIATELY unleashed a coordinated, preplanned political hit job against me. The Liberal Media spun up hit piece after hit piece to smear me in the press - Biden's former Press Secretary even attacked me for being a Christian on MSNBC. Politico fired off this headline: " Democrats rev up the opposition machine against Mike Johnson." And I can't even begin to tell you how many Democrat fundraising emails and texts I saw in the minutes following the final vote - I'm bracing myself for the next FEC reports. Look: These vicious attacks are a dime a dozen in Washington. They don't intimidate me. But I 'll let you in on a little secret: that's not really the goal... The real goal of this political ambush is two-fold: 1) smear the good names of every House Republican to hurt them electorally, and 2) scare Liberals enough to whip out their credit cards, fill in their info, and donate to Democrats. It 's about power and money. Nothing else. That's why it's mission-critical that we stand up for ourselves in critical moments like this. "So as one of my first moves as Speaker, I’m teaming up with my fellow Louisianian – Majority Leader Steve Scalise – to set a goal of raising one million dollars between now and the end of the month." It's ambitious. I get it. But that's the bare minimum we need to fully fund our campaign operations in battleground races across the country. If we fall short of that, we 'll be forced to SLASH our voter contact budgets, GUT our digital outreach efforts, and pull our ads OFF THE AIRWAVES. Nothing could be worse for our plans to defend and expand our slim House Majority. So here's my final pitch to you: We urgently need your help. Not next week, not in the new year - right here and right now. The difference between a strong Conservative Majority and one controlled by the Liberal Machine will come down to can't-miss fundraising goals just like this. Let me make myself perfectly clear: I 'm not asking you to pitch in more than you can afford. If you can spare $50 or $100, I would be tremendously grateful. But if you can only spare $5 or $10, I would be equally appreciative. "The ONLY thing I ask is for every Conservative in the country to chip in SOMETHING." That will be the difference between victory and defeat. So what do you say? Can I count you in? Thanks for all you do. In liberty, Speaker Mike Johnson | "Speaker Mike Johnson" | | | "Please take my Majority Leader Survey!" This is Steve Scalise. As your Majority Leader, I'm respectfully asking you to fill out my "MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY" at your earliest convenience. It's important. I want to have your goals, your concerns, and your priorities in mind before I do anything else. What would you like me to keep in mind when I'm taking on the Radical Democrats? | | "START MY MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY >>" | Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC. Team Scalise is committed to defending our Conservative House majority and electing Conservatives across the country. Thank you for being a part of our movement. Friend, election experts agree: **GOP senator Rick Scott is seriously vulnerable. And _CNN_ has ranked Florida as one of Democrats’ best pickup opportunities in 2024.**
This race couldn’t be more important: Florida is our best chance to save our Senate majority. Everything that we care about is on the line here: Lifesaving gun safety reform, the future of abortion rights, the strength of our democracy, our fight against climate change, and so much more.
The good news is, polling shows Debbie holding a narrow 1 point lead. And she even OUTRAISED Rick Scott in the last fundraising quarter! **All signs point to 2024 being the year that we****finally FLIP FLORIDA BLUE.**
**But Rick Scott pumped $64 million of his own money into the race to buy Florida’s Senate seat in 2018. And now, he's already shelled out over $3 MILLION to blanket the airwaves with attack ads.** The only way to compete with this self-funded extremist is to build an unstoppable grassroots war chest — and Debbie can’t do that without YOU.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jim=20Walsh?=
subject: Conservative Ballot Boxes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
Bringing back in person voting to Washington State... | | | [ ](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=2031a56069&e=6a281f566d)
---
| | _[_**Last email before the MIDNIGHT "freeze"**_](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=c0c38fc3ca&e=6a281f566d)_
Dear Republican friends,

The last item before I head into the legislative fundraising "freeze" - please make sure to continue supporting our efforts to**establish conservative voting centers **across the state.

Our team and I will continue to send out more information about how you can:

\- Be a trained grassroots conservative volunteer for ballot harvesting.
\- Help find conservative-leaning locations to establish voting centers.
\- Help register people to vote.
\- And more.

But, **before MIDNIGHT tonight,** I want to ask you one more time to consider sponsoring one of our Conservative Voting Centers**so we have the resources to get all 200 centers up and running before the 2024 elections are underway.**
---
| [Sponsor Conservative Voting Centers in Washington](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=d8f78e6bb2&e=6a281f566d "Sponsor Conservative Voting Centers in Washington")
---
| [$25, $50, or even the full $150](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=d4a4274745&e=6a281f566d) will get us a step closer to establishing these centers, **increasing Republican voter turnout** , and winning elections again for Republicans in Washington State!

These voting centers come fully stocked with:

\- Conservative secure ballot boxes
\- Trained and verified grassroots conservative teams
\- Election information
\- Individual Voter Information
\- Candidate information
\- And ballot tracking

Your support is critical to this program and this program is critical to Washington Republicans' efforts to**win again in Washington State.**

Thank you for your support,

Jim Walsh
Chairman
Washington State Republican Party
---
|
---
| To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Date: September 7, 2023
Time: 9:00 AM PST
From: Jim Walsh

Dear Peter,

One of the advantages of servings as both Chairman of the Washington State Republican Party and an elected State Representative in Olympia, is having thorough experiences and analysis in every corner of our great state. Providing a clear understanding of the key issues facing Washingtonians - and the solutions needed to solve our shared challenges.

One disadvantage, in my capacity as legislator,**I am prohibited** from directly making any campaign fundraising asks during the legislative "freeze" period, **which starts this Saturday.**

_I am legally able to fundraise for the State Party_ during this period, and while there are other chairs who are also elected officials across the country who do so, I will not be. We all know **Democrats and sections of the** **media here in Washington State would love nothing more than to create a false scandal around their favorite conservative target, me.

_You will still be hearing from me_ -** our critical work at the WAGOP will still continue** full steam ahead **over the next couple months - there is plenty of information and action items that I will be updating you with as we move forward. Unfortunately, **this will be the last opportunity I have for the next few months to ask you personally to fund our vital work.**

**[_To help fill the fundraising gap from now until the end of the legislative session, will you click the button below and make a year-end contribution for $25, $50, or even $100 to our operations today?_](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=263d207036&e=6a281f566d)**

Please consider making it your very best contribution this year as we will need every ounce of grassroots activism during the 2024 Legislative session.
---
| [Make an end of year contribution to the Republican Party](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=2c0e370eb5&e=6a281f566d "Make an end of year contribution to the Republican Party")
---
| I know that might be a lot to ask during the holiday season, but **we must take the fight directly to the Democrats starting in January** and I know you expect us to do so.

You can already tell how scared the Democrats are by the six initiatives that are on pace to qualify for the 2024 legislative session.

Our GOP teams will be there at the beginning of session making sure voters know Democrats have these common-sense initiatives in hand, ready to pass into law. We will be there throughout the legislative session to remind voters that **Democrats are refusing** to pass these initiatives into law. And we will be there after the session ends to tell voters how**Democrats failed to act** \- and that not only will we pass these initiatives at the ballot box in November, but we will replace the Democrat legislators who failed to act.

Along with efforts to support common-sense initiatives signed by citizens, **the State Party will also have the task** of mobilizing grassroots conservatives to register opinions on bills, contact legislators who are on the wrong side of common-sense legislation, and once-again making sure voters across the state know Democrats are the ones responsible for the failures of our state.

**Without your vital support,** our teams cannot carry out this important work during the legislative session
---
| [Make an end of year contribution to the Republican Party](https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=2da70106db&e=6a281f566d "Make an end of year contribution to the Republican Party")
---
|
---
| | Republicans in Washington State need to win the early battles in January if we want to win the close races in November. We will do it as aggressively as possible. And in doing so, **we will start winning again** come November 2024.

Please support our efforts today and let's head into 2024 united and ready to work. Thank you!

Sincerely,

Jim Walsh
Chairman
Washington State Republican Party
---
| | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/wsrp/smooth-transition-1341760?e=6a281f566d)
---

We all know Democrats and sections of the media here in Washington State would love nothing more than to create a false scandal around their favorite conservative target, me. You will still be hearing from me - our critical work at the WAGOP will still continue full steam ahead over the next couple months - there is plenty of information and action items that I will be updating you with as we move forward. Unfortunately, this will be the last opportunity I have for the next few months to ask you personally to fund our vital work. Please consider making it your very best contribution this year as we will need every ounce of grassroots activism during the 2024 Legislative session. | "Make an end of year contribution to the Republican Party" | I know that might be a lot to ask during the holiday season, but we must take the fight directly to the Democrats starting in January and I know you expect us to do so. You can already tell how scared the Democrats are by the six initiatives that are on pace to qualify for the 2024 legislative session. Our GOP teams will be there at the beginning of session making sure voters know Democrats have these common-sense initiatives in hand, ready to pass into law. We will be there throughout the legislative session to remind voters that Democrats are refusing to pass these initiatives into law. And we will be there after the session ends to tell voters howDemocrats failed to act \- and that not only will we pass these initiatives at the ballot box in November, but we will replace the Democrat legislators who failed to act. Along with efforts to support common-sense initiatives signed by citizens, the State Party will also have the task of mobilizing grassroots conservatives to register opinions on bills, contact legislators who are on the wrong side of common-sense legislation, and once-again making sure voters across the state know Democrats are the ones responsible for the failures of our state. Without your vital support, our teams cannot carry out this important work during the legislative session | "Make an end of year contribution to the Republican Party" | | | Republicans in Washington State need to win the early battles in January if we want to win the close races in November. We will do it as aggressively as possible. And in doing so, we will start winning again come November 2024. Please support our efforts today and let's head into 2024 united and ready to work. Thank you! Sincerely, Jim Walsh Chairman Washington State Republican Party | | | Copyright © 2023 Washington State Republican Party, All rights reserved. Your interest in the Washington State Republican Party, National Republican Committee or Washington state Republican Candidates Our mailing address is: Washington State Republican Party11811 NE 1st St Suite 306Bellevue, WA 98005 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or . | | Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party. 11811 NE 1st St, Bellevue, WA 98005. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

**Look around, Fellow American. Right now, in America...**
_Children are being taught in many schools to be ashamed of their country and themselves._

_The pledge is being taken out of school because it mentions God._

_Free speech is being censored and silenced right before our eyes._

_Democrats are fighting for the right to elective abortion right up to the moment of birth._

_Unelected, unaccountable bureaucrats are seeking control of almost every aspect of our lives._

**As Speaker of the House, my mission is clear: we must elect more leaders who make decisions based on American values with an emphasis on God, family, and country. **

As we aim to make history in the 2024 Election, I need to have my hand on the pulse of the most important thing: _**American voters.**_

disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MAHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/maherforcongress/opening-day-12776692?e=1722d05e10)
---

---
| |
Friend, The Chinese Communist Party (CCP) is here in our backyard, buying our farms, poisoning our communities, and compromising our politicians.

Funding covert bio-labs, drying up our farmland, flooding us with fentanyl, and Governor Gavin Newsom is funneling hundreds of millions of tax dollars to a Chinese electric car company for plastic face masks.

CCP is the leading and most consequential threat to U.S. national security.

A generation of lifestyle career politicians like Joe Biden and Jim Costa have abandoned us to China’s interests.

Influencing forces have created a water and energy crisis by starving our farmland of water, ripping out our trees, and turning the naked earth into solar and battery fields from China.

I know this threat. I directly faced it as a FBI Special Agent.

The threat is here, and it is real.

We can turn this around; I see a path forward. I have a vision for our future born from my real-world and military experiences. [Please consider a $5, $10, or $20 donation](https://michaelmaher.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6239b7d9cb3ad9388556be8be&id=e81a421890&e=1722d05e10) to support our fight against this threat.
---

| Or, [please consider a weekly $5 donation](https://michaelmaher.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6239b7d9cb3ad9388556be8be&id=936fd46544&e=1722d05e10) to help this campaign bring new law enforcement and military veteran experience to our Central Valley and nation.

Thank you in advance.

Sincerely,
---
|
---
| **Michael Maher**
Businessman | Veteran | Republican for Congress

|

| _**Background** : Michael Maher is a former FBI Special Agent and US Navy Veteran who was raised in the Central Valley. name: Tom Fitton
subject: Judicial Watch Victory! DC Cleans Voter Rolls!
body:
More Censorship Exposed!

[INSIDE JW]

DC Removing 103,000 Ineligible Names from Voter Rolls in Response to
Judicial Watch

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30874&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdmlkZW9zL3ZpY3RvcnktZGMtY2xlYW5zLWVsZWN0aW9uLXJvbGxzLWFsc28taHVudGVyLWluZGljdGVkLWFnYWluLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=9ba313db99cb8b7a979c19517f60a8cb39a114af0e4671d6a3d39e586f85911f]
We’ve achieved another significant victory in our fight to clean
up the nation’s voter rolls – this time in our nation’s capital.

We sent notice letters
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30860&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL252cmEtbm90aWNlcy1vZi12aW9sYXRpb24tbm92ZW1iZXItMjAyMy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=6ab3ae968c6a5530a975a00ab10b624f6fb91f0fc74382eb53ea88ff39755b2c]
to election officials in the District of Columbia, California, and
Illinois, notifying them of evident violations of the National Voter
Registration Act
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=2071&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvY3J0L25hdGlvbmFsLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi1hY3QtMTk5My1udnJhXShOVlJBP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=a4bdbeaaf8d73928cdb262b0faba827be4b73b62cc461f035197a39f5c77f959)
of 1993, based on their failure to remove inactive voters from their
registration rolls.

The letters point out that these jurisdictions publicly reported
removing few or no ineligible voter registrations under a key
provision of the NVRA. The letters threaten federal lawsuits unless
the violations are corrected in a timely fashion.

In response to our inquiries, Washington, DC, officials admitted that
they had not complied with the NVRA, promptly removed 65,544 outdated
names from the voting rolls, promised to remove 37,962 more, and
designated another 73,522 registrations as “inactive.”

The NVRA requires states to “conduct a general program that makes a
reasonable effort to remove” from the official voter rolls “the
names of ineligible voters” who have died or changed residence. The
law requires registrations to be canceled when voters fail to respond
to address confirmation notices and then fail to vote in the next two
general federal elections. In 2018, the Supreme Court confirmed that
such removals are mandatory (_Husted v. A. Philip Randolph Inst_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=26310&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8xN3BkZi8xNi05ODBfZjJxMy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1d1244a9a7e3186f078e968636ba0553fa3d9278af94361dcddcd24ee326f4bb].,
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=26310&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8xN3BkZi8xNi05ODBfZjJxMy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1d1244a9a7e3186f078e968636ba0553fa3d9278af94361dcddcd24ee326f4bb]
138 S.
Ct. 1833, 1841-42 (2018)).

Federal law directs the Election Assistance Commission (EAC) to submit
a report to Congress every second year assessing how states are
complying with the NVRA. Federal regulations require states to provide
data to the EAC for use in this report. On June 29, 2023, the EAC
posted data from the most recent surveys it sent to the states and the
District of Columbia for the reporting period from November 2020
through November 2022.

Based on the data contained in this report, we contacted a number of
states and Washington, DC, to inquire about their compliance with
federal law and to request public records. After processing the
responses to these communications, Judicial Watch sent notice of
violation letters to Washington, DC, California, and Illinois,
detailing their non-compliance with the NVRA.

The notice letter
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30861&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL252cmEtZGMtbm90aWNlLW9mLXZpb2xhdGlvbi1ub3ZlbWJlci0yMDIzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=8f8d3158196a2356c4169a3c5432333ea51eafae164abd2d3df78362aca39a70]
to the District of Columbia, sent on behalf of Judicial Watch and the
District of Columbia Republican Party, reports:

> DC reported removing _zero _voter registrations in the last
> _two-year_ reporting period for failing to respond to an address
> confirmation notice and failing to vote for two consecutive general
> federal elections.

* DC flatly admitted in correspondence with Judicial
Watch that it was failing to remove registrations as required by the
NVRA, citing data conversion, staffing, and other issues.
* DC’s total registration rate—its total number of
registrations divided by the most recent census estimates of its
citizen voting-age population—is greater than 131%.

The notice letter
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30862&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL252cmEtY2Etbm90aWNlLW9mLXZpb2xhdGlvbi1ub3ZlbWJlci0yMDIzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=2462ff34dc690363403528ba9f87025741f634062406d16bfeacdaaa8fd57a31]
to California, sent on behalf of Judicial Watch and the Libertarian
Party of California, states:

* California’s survey responses to the EAC show that 27
California counties reported removing five or fewer—and, in most of
those counties, zero—voter registrations in the last two-year period
for failing to respond to an address confirmation notice and failing
to vote in two consecutive general federal elections.
* Another 19 California counties simply did not report
any data about such removals.
* Twenty-one California counties had more voter
registrations than citizens over the age of 18, based on the most
recent census estimates.

In all, 46 California counties reported removing only a handful, or
no registrations under the NVRA’s change of address rules, or else
failed to report any data at all. These 46 counties contain more than
14 million registered voters.

The notice letter
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30863&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL252cmEtaWwtbm90aWNlLW9mLXZpb2xhdGlvbi1ub3ZlbWJlci0yMDIzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=2d3f2beaa4e82c42e0ead8891e3a5d0ad2ff6df797ea5b45e9a998c66afb3ddf]
to Illinois, sent on behalf of Judicial Watch, Illinois resident and
voter Carol J. Davis and Illinois Family Action, states:

* In Illinois’ responses to the EAC’s survey, 23
Illinois counties reported removing fewer than 15—and, in almost
half of those counties, zero—voter registrations from November 2020
to November 2022 for failing to respond to an address confirmation
notice and failing to vote in two consecutive general federal
elections.
* Thirty-four Illinois jurisdictions simply did not
report any data about such removals.
* Fifteen Illinois jurisdictions have more voter
registrations than citizens of voting age.

In total, 57 Illinois counties either reported removing 15 or fewer
registrations or failed to report any data at all under the NVRA’s
change of address removal procedures. These 57 counties contain over
five million registered voters.

Last month, the District of Columbia admitted in correspondence
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30864&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL252cmEtbm92ZW1iZXItMjAyMy1kYy1yZXNwb25zZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=cd680d5c92ad8348ca9564f96461e4d368af6c2d4ca19ae106e2a52a1ad6cacf]
to us that it removed 65,544 inactive voters, will soon remove an
additional 37,962 inactive voters, and that it has designated another
73,522 inactive names for potential removal.

California provided some public records and promised a further,
substantive response. Illinois provided some public records and
promises a further, substantive response. Ultimately, however, if
Judicial Watch is not satisfied with the jurisdictions’ responses to
its notice letters, Judicial Watch plans to sue under the National
Voter Registration Act to ensure the jurisdictions take certain
reasonable steps to clean up their voter rolls as the law requires.

Dirty voter rolls increase the potential for voter fraud. As
Washington, DC’s, quick cleanup of tens of thousands of names in
response to us shows, there are potentially hundreds of thousands of
names on the voter rolls that should be removed by California and
Illinois. Indeed, our litigation resulted in the removal of four
million names from voter rolls in various states recently.”

We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As
part of our work, we assembled a team of highly experienced voting
rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and
cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky,
among other achievements
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=21208&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL252cmEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6895639061ab0e9dd273a0f81e79d0c7c0d5d5fea1149f126ef741e00ac8c21d].
Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election
law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil
Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting
rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements
in dozens of states.

JUDICIAL WATCH TAXPAYER LAWSUIT OVER RACIALLY DISCRIMINATORY
MINNEAPOLIS TEACHERS’ CONTRACT TO PROCEED

In another major victory for Judicial Watch, the Minnesota Court of
Appeals reversed a trial court’s ruling and is allowing our historic
lawsuit filed on behalf of a Minneapolis taxpayer over a teachers’
contract that provides discriminatory job protections to certain
racial minorities to proceed (_Clapp v. Cox et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30834&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2NsYXBwLXYtY294LW1pbm5lc290YS10ZWFjaGVycy1hMjMtMDM2MC0xMjQ1NC9dXz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=bc9f9faed62f608f585d1ef9f0147482516163aaf0f3879bb8d916958325e6f7
_(No. 27-CV-22-12454)).

Hennepin County District Court had dismissed the lawsuit, ruling that
our taxpayer client lacked standing and that her claims were not ripe.
We appealed and, on December 4, the Minnesota Court of Appeals
overturned the lower court’s decision, ruling that our client does
have standing as a taxpayer who helps fund Minneapolis Public Schools
through property taxes and her claims are ripe because the lawsuit
alleges an actual future controversy using public funds.

Judicial Watch filed the lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=27927&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdC1vdmVyLWRpc2NyaW1pbmF0b3J5LWNvbnRyYWN0Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=7998243dccd7e003c9e52dc55476e7ac7632f3a66eafa562b8ea40dcb08e875f]
in August of 2022 against the superintendent of the Minneapolis Public
Schools, the Minneapolis Public Schools (MPS), and the Minneapolis
Board of Education for violating the Equal Protection Guarantee of the
Minnesota Constitution.

The controversial contract was agreed to in March 2022 to end a 14-day
teacher strike. The Minneapolis Federation of Teachers ratified the
contract shortly after the agreement was reached. The Minneapolis
Board of Education ratified it in May of this year.
Our lawsuit states:

> Among other things, the contract provides preferences, protections,
> and privileges for MPS teachers of certain races and ethnicities
> under a section entitled “ARTICLE 15. PROTECTIONS FOR EDUCATORS OF
> COLOR.” There is no similar provision covering educators who are
> not “of color.”
>
> Under the contract, teachers of color are exempt from Defendant
> MPS’s seniority-based layoffs and reassignments, which means, when
> layoffs or reassignments occur, the next senior teacher who is not
> “of color” would be laid off or reassigned. In addition, the
> contract mandates that Defendants reinstate teachers of color over
> more senior teachers who are not “of color.”
>
> Upon information and belief, prior to the contract, teachers were
> laid off or reassigned in order of seniority, with the least senior
> teachers laid off or reassigned first, without regard to race or
> ethnicity. Similarly, teachers were reinstated in order of
> seniority, with the more senior teachers reinstated first, without
> regard to race or ethnicity.

***

> Article 15’s preferences, protections, and privileges for certain
> public-school teachers on the basis of race and ethnicity violates
> Minnesota’s Equal Protection Guarantee, which states that “no
> member of this state shall be disenfranchised or deprived of any of
> the rights or privileges secured to any citizen thereof, unless by
> the law of the land or the judgment of his peers.” Minn. Const.
> art. 1, § 2. The Equal Protection Guarantee is analyzed under the
> same principles and mandate as the Equal Protection Clause of the
> U.S. Constitution.
In its December 4 ruling, the Minnesota Court of Appeals states:

> [The] complaint states that, before the adoption of Article 15,
> “teachers were laid off or reassigned in order of seniority . . .
> , without regard to race or ethnicity.” Teachers were also
> “reinstated in order of seniority, with the more senior teachers
> reinstated first, without regard to race or ethnicity.” But the
> complaint alleges that Article 15 of the March 2022 agreement
> altered this practice, such that “teachers of color are exempt
> from [the] seniority-based layoffs and reassignments, which means,
> when layoffs or reassignments occur, the next senior teacher who is
> not ‘of color’ would be laid off or reassigned.” Article 15
> states:
>
> Starting with the Spring 2023 Budget Tie-Out Cycle, if excessing a
> teacher who is a member of a population underrepresented among
> licensed teachers in the site, the [school district] shall excess
> the next least senior teacher, who is not a member of an
> underrepresented population, for the reasons provided in Article
> 15.1.2.i.

***

> Reinstatement must be in the inverse order of placement on lay off.
> The [school district] shall prioritize the recall of a teacher who
> is a member of a population underrepresented among licensed teachers
> in the [school district], per the definition provided in Article
> 15.1.2.i. To do this, the [school district] shall deprioritize the
> more senior teacher, who is not a member of an underrepresented
> population, in order to recall a teacher who is a member of an
> underrepresented population among licensed teachers, for the reasons
> provided in Article 15.1.2.i.

> Article 15.1.2.i provides that the anticipated outcome of the policy
> is to:
>
> remedy the continuing effects of past discrimination by the [school
> district]. Past discrimination by the [school district]
> disproportionately impacted the hiring of underrepresented teachers
> in the [school district], as compared to the relevant labor market
> and the community, and resulted in a lack of diversity of teachers.
> Language which refers to this Article will no longer be in effect
> once the teachers in the [school district] reflect the diversity of
> the labor market and the community served by the [school district].

> The complaint alleges that MPS will lay off or reassign
> approximately 220 teachers between 2022 and 2027.
>
> According to [the] complaint, Article 15 violates equal protection
> under the state constitution because it “provides preferences,
> protections, and privileges for MPS teachers of certain races and
> ethnicities” for layoffs and recalls. The complaint further
> asserts that Article 15 requires MPS to spend public money to
> implement this allegedly unlawful practice.
The lawsuit will go back to Hennepin County District Court for
further proceedings.

The lawsuit asks the court to enter a judgment declaring all actions
taken to implement the racial and ethnic preference provisions of
Article 15 of the contract to be illegal. The lawsuit also asks that
the court declare illegal the use of any taxpayer dollars to implement
these provisions of the contract and that MPS be prohibited from
taking any actions to implement these racial and ethnic provisions.

This is a big victory for taxpayers who are outraged that
Minneapolis’ school system would engage in blatant racial
discrimination in employing teachers. We will move with all due speed
to gather evidence in discovery. This lawsuit aims to shut down this
extreme leftist attack on the bedrock constitutional principle that no
one can be denied equal treatment under the law on account of race.

We are being assisted in the lawsuit by Daniel N. Rosen of Rosen LLC
in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

The City of Asheville, NC, in January 2022 settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=26643&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctdmljdG9yeS1hc2hldmlsbGUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=a80dae9ae9cdd58fb0ef02a98266153d491f6ff898bcc838029d545a80ff6121]
our federal
civil rights lawsuit after agreeing to remove all racially
discriminatory provisions in a city-funded scholarship program.
Additionally, the city also agreed to remove racially discriminatory
eligibility provisions in a related program that provides grants to
educators.

In May 2022, we won a court battle
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=27378&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY291cnQtZGVjbGFyZXMtdW5jb25zdGl0dXRpb25hbC1jYWxpZm9ybmlhLWdlbmRlci1xdW90YS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=d770c41115ddb7b1066f8541d3a1a4ce9b39ae231a6571e1d78d1d99c6d29454]
against California’s gender quota law for corporate boards. The
verdict came after a 28-day trial. The verdict followed a similar

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=27068&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY2EtY291cnQtb3Bpbmlvbi1leHBsYWlucy1oaXN0b3JpYy1ydWxpbmcvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=10486a90f0d1b494d339528c63ba83606b69cc5f81843d2a0ba4c3dbae154057]

MORE EVIDENCE OF GOVERNMENT CENSORSHIP DURING 2020 ELECTION

We received 44 pages
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30837&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGhzLWVsZWN0aW9uLWludGVncml0eS1wYXJ0bmVyc2hpcC1wcm9kLTEtcmVjb3Jkcy0wMzU2MC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=5e8dd3b94e2aa49a0af59e34de3e78623dd745a48509a26360a55796a1efb5be]
of records in a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit from the
U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) that show a close
collaboration between DHS’s Cybersecurity and Information Security
Agency (CISA) and the leftist Election Integrity Partnership (EIP) to
engage in “real-time narrative tracking” on all major social media
platforms in the days leading up to the 2020 election.

The records discuss “takedowns” of social media posts and the
avoidance of creating public records subject to FOIA.

These records show the lengths to which a ‘Homeland Security’ Deep
State agency went in its effort to censor and suppress Americans
during and after the 2020 election. That it took a federal lawsuit to
extract these disturbing records should raise additional worries about
what else this Biden administration is up to.

The records also show that the EIP, which was initially called the
Election Misinformation Partnership in the days leading up to the
November 3, 2020, election, tasked staffers with monitoring online
election content 24 hours a day with a priority being “disinfo that
is going viral.”

We obtained the records thanks to our FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=28502&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGhzLWNlbnNvcnNoaXAtcmVxdWVzdHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6de7cd770c319985aec4b4e64cf7080991aeff206eea30e1bc622dace3e0063e]
after DHS
failed to respond to our October 5, 2022, request (_Judicial Watch v.
U.S. Department of Homeland Security_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30838&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGhzLWVsZWN0aW9uLWludGVncml0eS1wYXJ0bmVyc2hpcC1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDM1NjAvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4360fbdec88871ded4c33f46e806954828d49c7dbc34e6cb58a24f7756ae1348

The Election Integrity Partnership
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=28509&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9jeWJlci5mc2kuc3RhbmZvcmQuZWR1L2lvL25ld3MvYW5ub3VuY2luZy1lbGVjdGlvbi1pbnRlZ3JpdHktcGFydG5lcnNoaXA/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=03e1023b81d3b2ba4f37e5316b1dc8dfeeb5783c6296904369b30b92c8373083]
was created
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=28509&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9jeWJlci5mc2kuc3RhbmZvcmQuZWR1L2lvL25ld3MvYW5ub3VuY2luZy1lbGVjdGlvbi1pbnRlZ3JpdHktcGFydG5lcnNoaXA/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=03e1023b81d3b2ba4f37e5316b1dc8dfeeb5783c6296904369b30b92c8373083]
in July 2020, just before the presidential election. According
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=28510&F=T&&hash=ce554eb88a26816a9cbd8e4b7b7a836633946b9989770709f619558d98b88eaf]
to _Just the News_:

> The consortium is comprised of four member organizations: Stanford
> Internet Observatory (SIO), the University of Washington’s Center
> for an Informed Public, the Atlantic Council’s Digital Forensic
> Research Lab, and social media analytics firm Graphika. It set up a
> concierge-like service in 2020 that allowed federal agencies like
> Homeland’s Cybersecurity Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) and
> State’s Global Engagement Center to file “tickets” requesting
> that online story links and social media posts be censored or
> flagged by Big Tech.
>
> Three liberal groups — the Democratic National Committee, Common
> Cause and the NAACP — were also empowered like the federal
> agencies to file tickets seeking censorship of content. A
> Homeland-funded collaboration, the Elections Infrastructure

> Best way to collaborate
> What's the best way to collaborate?

* CISA can't create their own Slack channels, but can
participate in others'.

* Listservs are bad (public records requirements).

* Jira is fine.
* CISA has privacy concerns: can't monitor people's
individual accounts; ensure CISA doesn't participate in discussions or
notes concerning U.S. persons.

* Setup:

* SIO will have dedicated Slack, something like Jira or
Salesforce (will ask for donation), separate from Stanford and
destroyed once over.
* We'll intake info by email, but direct people to
private forms SIO and CISA have distributed.
* Info from there will go into queue -> be triaged,
assigned SLA.

* Mutual trust is key: don't want to need NDAs, legal red
tape.

* Need to build out workflow management system:
JIRA/Slack/other communications channels, shared processes and
definitions, etc.

* [Redacted] envisions Tier 1 and Tier 2 partners:

* Tier 1 is intake (of tips, disinfo reports, etc.):
consisting of people either digging for narratives, or processing info
received from other partners.

* Think students, election officials, etc. who are looking for
disinfo.
* Workflow: check that info against protocols, do some initial data
aggregation, triage it into the workflow management system.

* Tier 2 is the 4 orgs [redacted] I team at Stanford, Graphika, etc.

* Workflow: take stuff off the workflow management queue, process
it.
* Need to sketch that out.
* SLA for different times of the calendar based on the level of
severity obtained by triage.
* E.g., a report from the general public will have less priority
than a report from an on-the-ground election official; a report for
disinfo that is not popular will have less priority than disinfo that
is going viral.
* General public = more turnaround time, but election officials =
less turnaround time: need to get back to them fast.

* SIO has good relationship w/ platforms who already care.

* See the Secondary Infektion (Russian disinfo op) report.

* Think through all the platforms that might have been useful there
(e.g., communicating with Twitter at stage x would have stopped the
spread).

* Meanwhile, CISA has strong relationships w/ election officials.

* CISA is happy to introduce SIO to them, do outreach.
* Just keep CISA in the info-sharing pipeline.

***

> CISA’s concern starts 45 days out operationally, when
> military/overseas voters start mailing.

* Start hunting, messaging at beginning of September.

* Lower SLA (higher turnaround time/less priority), but
start looking for search terms and taking tips.

* The days leading up to/right after Election Day will be
much more intense.
* It'll be an effective SOC, maybe a physical one, but in
a much larger space.

> Notes: This scenario has a geographical component, but seems

In a June 26, 2023 report
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66131&L=30852&F=T&&hash=96e781dd973743c96c8fb244d1df583c41871ba7afb52c3a20ca1b967339de79],
the Committee on the Judiciary and the Select Subcommittee on the
Weaponization of the Federal Government writes about CISA:

> Founded in 2018, CISA was originally intended to be an ancillary
> agency designed to protect “critical infrastructure” and guard
> against cybersecurity threats. In the years since its creation,
> however, CISA metastasized into the nerve center of the federal
> government’s domestic surveillance and censorship operations on
> social media. By 2020, CISA routinely reported social media posts
> that allegedly spread “disinformation” to social media
> platforms. By 2021, CISA had a formal “Mis-, Dis-, and
> Malinformation” (MDM) team. In 2022 and 2023, in response to
> growing public and private criticism of CISA’s unconstitutional
> behavior, CISA attempted to camouflage its activities, duplicitously
> claiming it serves a purely “informational” role.

> security threats created by the Biden administration’s disastrous
> open border policies. The crucial information only surfaced and

> what the FBI is doing to address the threats while adhering to the

> He mentioned the FBI’s concern about the Taliban takeover of

> closed by asking Congress to reauthorize a section of the Foreign
